codeReview.summary: Clean implementation of the New Game button feature. The worker extracted a shared seed generation utility (src/lib/seed.ts) with two functions — generateRandomSeed and generateDifferentSeed — replacing duplicate implementations in game.tsx and index.tsx. The handleNewGame callback navigates to a new seed URL, which naturally resets all derived state (marks, bingo, celebration) because marks are keyed by seed in sessionStorage and bingo is computed reactively from marks. All 7 verification assertions from the handoff were browser-verified, and 9 unit tests cover the seed generation logic. The implementation is correct and complete.

issues.description: Modulo bias in generateRandomSeed: `byte % 62` gives chars 0-5 (A-F) a probability of 5/256 vs 4/256 for other chars, since 256 is not evenly divisible by 62. This is negligible for game seeds (62^10 space makes it irrelevant) and consistent with the pattern already in use before this commit, but technically not uniform. Could be fixed with rejection sampling if ever needed.

generateDifferentSeed silently returns potentially same seed after maxAttempts (10) exhausted. With 62^10 ≈ 8.4×10^17 space this is astronomically impossible, but a defensive fallback (e.g., append a counter or throw) would make the guarantee explicit. No functional impact.

sharedStateObservations.observation: src/lib/seed.ts is a new shared utility file not documented in AGENTS.md Key Files or .factory/library/architecture.md. The architecture doc lists bingo.ts and items.ts under Game Logic but omits seed.ts. Future workers may not discover the shared seed generation utility.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md 'Key Files' section lists src/lib/bingo.ts and src/lib/items.ts but not src/lib/seed.ts. architecture.md Components > Game Logic section only mentions bingo.ts and items.ts.

summary: The New Game button feature is correctly implemented. The shared seed utility (src/lib/seed.ts) properly generates random seeds and guarantees they differ from the current seed. The handleNewGame callback navigates to a new seed URL, and since marks are sessionStorage-keyed by seed and bingo is reactively derived from marks, all state (marks, bingo, celebration) naturally resets on navigation. Rapid clicking works because seed generation is synchronous and each navigate call is independent. Browser back button returns to the previous seed URL with marks restored from sessionStorage. Landing page Play generates a fresh seed each click via the shared utility. Two non-blocking issues noted: minor modulo bias in seed generation (negligible for this use case) and theoretical possibility of generateDifferentSeed returning same seed after 10 attempts (astronomically unlikely). One shared state observation: the new seed.ts utility should be documented in architecture.md.
